description:
## Background

Workspace selection cannot safely be layered over `std::env::current_dir()` or `env::set_current_dir()`: the desktop process cwd is global, while Beadsmith can have asynchronous work in flight. The application needs one explicit, testable backend boundary that owns workspace state and supplies a working directory to every Beadwork subprocess.

This is an intentional expand-stage prefactor. It creates the new path-scoped workspace contract beside the current launch-cwd path so the repository remains buildable while the following slice moves the product UI to it. It is not a second, user-facing workspace mode.

## What to build

Introduce the backend Workspace foundation: a persisted Workspace Catalog, optional Current Workspace, Pending Workspace, validation of a candidate directory to its Beadwork Git root, and explicit working-directory threading from the workspace service through the Issue Explorer loader and `CommandRunner`. Expose typed workspace operations and state through TauRPC while keeping caller-supplied paths out of issue-loading operations.

## Technical Approach

- Add backend-owned Tauri store support, using one versioned JSON catalog and explicit `save()` calls as durability boundaries. Do not grant frontend store access.
- Register the Rust store plugin. Add a command-runner API that takes an explicit working directory; make the new adapter/load path require it for All Issues, Ready, and Blocked.
- Validate candidates by running `bw config list` in the candidate directory and independently discover the Git repository root. A successful Beadwork command from a subdirectory must be normalized to the repository root before catalog identity, loading, or persistence.
- Model known, Current, and Pending Workspace state in a cohesive backend module. Define typed TauRPC operations to read workspace state, switch or retry a candidate path, and remove a known Workspace. The new Issue Explorer load operation reads only backend-held Current Workspace.
- Preserve the existing launch-cwd implementation only as a temporary compatibility path until the initial-selection slice migrates every caller; do not broaden it.

## Considerations

- Store mutation alone is not a successful switch: future slices must use `save()` before publishing a new Current Workspace.
- Normal path handling defines catalog identity in v1. Do not add symlink canonicalization or alias deduplication.
- This slice must distinguish invalid/non-Beadwork directories from later Beadwork loading failures.
- Keep the state-service API narrow and cohesive so later UI code does not learn persistence or subprocess details.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A backend workspace service owns typed known, pending, and optional current state without using process cwd for its new load path.
- [ ] Candidate validation identifies and returns the Beadwork Git root; a selected subdirectory cannot become a separate Workspace identity.
- [ ] All three Beadwork issue-view commands can run through `CommandRunner` with an explicit working directory.
- [ ] TauRPC exposes typed workspace state and operations while Issue Explorer loading accepts no frontend workspace path.
- [ ] Store access is backend-owned and explicit save failures are representable to callers.
- [ ] Existing application behavior remains green until the next slice removes the compatibility path.

## Test Plan

- Rust unit tests for candidate validation, Git-root normalization, store serialization, and missing/invalid paths.
- Runner and adapter tests proving all view commands receive the requested cwd and no new code calls `env::current_dir()`.
- TauRPC contract tests and TypeScript-binding generation checks.
- Run `cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, and TypeScript checking.
title: Establish the path-scoped Workspace foundation
